rising.title: OpenAI Developments
rising.latest_event: Alibaba has reportedly banned employees from using Claude Code (idx 8), adding a corporate security/competitive dimension to the Cursor-at-SpaceX story from last week — both cases involve frontier AI coding tools being restricted by large organizations, suggesting a pattern of institutional friction with external AI coding assistants.

rising.title: The Closed-Source LLM Business Model Under Pressure: Cost, Competition, and Sustainability
rising.latest_event: Bridgewater's finding that GPT and Claude failed its proprietary finance tests because correct answers were never public (idx 21) is a pointed data point for the closed-source value proposition debate — it suggests frontier models are effective at reproducing publicly available knowledge but fail at genuinely novel institutional intelligence, precisely the use case enterprises pay premium prices to solve.

rising.title: AI's Labor Impact: Labs Signal End of Junior Knowledge Work
rising.latest_event: A 26,000-student study finds that AI's hidden learning costs take two full years to surface (idx 15), adding a longitudinal evidence dimension to the labor displacement story — the productivity gains that are displacing junior knowledge workers may simultaneously be degrading the pipeline that produces future senior workers.

rising.title: Benchmark Integrity Crisis: Reward Hacking and the SWE-bench Problem
rising.latest_event: A new item directly examines how agentic test processes interact with LLM benchmarks (idx 19), and a separate finding that AI search agents fail not at searching but at asking the right questions when queries are ambiguous (idx 20) adds a new failure-mode dimension: benchmarks that test retrieval quality may be missing the more fundamental query-formulation problem.

rising.title: AI Agent Infrastructure: The Emerging Memory and State Management Layer
rising.latest_event: DuoMem (idx 23), a dual-space distillation approach for on-device memory agents, extends the AI agent memory infrastructure story into the on-device inference domain — a new deployment constraint that the prior cloud-centric memory architecture debate had not addressed.

rising.title: AI in Scientific Review and Research Automation
rising.latest_event: Claude Science Beta is now formally described as a multi-agent workbench for reproducible genomics, proteomics, and cheminformatics pipelines (idx 17), giving the product a specific scientific domain footprint that will enable direct comparison with Google's Paper Assistant and other competitors.
